purpose: Identifies the inconsistency between equity-implied and rates-implied nominal GDP growth to determine which market is mispriced. Answers: are equities or rates correct about the growth outlook?
components: Equity-implied NGDP: S&P at ~21x fwd P/E with ~10% EPS growth implies 5-6%
Rates-implied NGDP: forwards embedding 75-100bp easing imply 3-4%
HY OAS at 350-420bp as a tiebreaker (leans equity narrative)
Causal interpretation: equities price fiscal-sustained economy, rates price private-sector-only economy
Historical base rate: ~60-65% rates correct, but imprecise post-COVID
current_assessment: The divergence is real and unsustainable. The macro_strategist_02 interpretation (equities and rates are each correct about different economies — the fiscal-sustained vs. private-sector-only) won debate as the cleanest causal explanation. Resolution determines whether HY spreads are fairly valued or 100-200bp too tight.
limitations: ~60-65% historical base rate favoring rates is insufficiently sourced and may be impaired post-COVID
Direction of resolution remains low confidence (6/10)
HY OAS as tiebreaker may itself be distorted by private credit and covenant-lite dynamics
